reviews.review_description: I am enjoying this alot.  People day vr doesn't add to the game but I disagree.  This game is meant to be played as is so I like the depth vr adds to the visuals and it makes for a chill game I can play on my couch and relax.  I would recommend if you like the flat version or just looking for a game to play seated.

Vampire survivors is a game I thought I would never play. The moment I started playing it, I couldn’t stop. And that was just on consoles.

When it came to VR, I was delighted. I’ve seen in the comments that this didn’t turn out the way some people thought it would. I disagree. I absolutely love how the game is presented in the headset. Highly recommend you pick this up and give it a try. It’s a blast and very hard to put down.

So why did I almost give it five stars. Because there’s a wealth of content that wasn’t included. There’s a lot more DLC and updates that have been released for console that I’ve yet to see making an appearance on VR. Including the Castlevania stuff. And that’s a true disappointment to me. Cause I would love to play that stuff.
Please give me more DLCs. I'm addicted and need more stuff. It's a great game and better in VR! My Jeff Minter eye is satisfied. Take my money!

I am a huge fan of the original game which I bought for all my consoles including all DLCs, so I immediately got this version out of excitement. Unfortunately I was quickly disappointed to see how basic the VR implementation is, as it follows almost no standard or guideline on what makes a quality VR game. The list of notes would be pretty long to write here.

The full Vampire Survivors experience is indeed there, but it just feels quickly slapped inside a work in progress wrapper app. The idea is good, but execution needs a lot more work than this. It just doesn’t do justice to the original game and feels like a huge missed opportunity.

reviews.review_title: Full Game In VR but Needs a Little More
reviews.review_description:
I was tempted into purchasing this game on yet another platform!  The VR gameplay is like watching a 2-D stage show from above.  The rest of the screen is filled with animated graphics that look neat.

I hate playing with the Quest controller though.  I have an 8bitdo controller connected via Bluetooth and I can move around with that but I still have to make choices and navigate menus with the Quest controller.

It really needs to be fully controller compatible before I can put in long play hours.  

Other than the controller problem I really find this version loaded with charm!

reviews.review_title: A great way to play Vampire Survivors
reviews.review_description:
At launch it lacked some polish and could be buggy setting up your play session, but when you get going, it's what you expect: Vampire Survivors in a VR immersive environment.

If I could ask for one thing, I'd just like the developer to implement microgestures for controller-free gameplay.

It's Vampire Survivors, the classic game on a flatscreen panel in front of you with a 3D environment around you. If you're expecting a 3D game mode it's nothing new, this is classic Vampire Survivors. Pros & Cons.


Pros:
- Classic Vampire Survivors
- Interactive background.

Cons:
- No mixed reality mode
- Performance can dip sometimes, using QGO helps

This isn't a VR version of the game. It's just the console game rendered on a flat screen within a static VR environment. So the only reason to own this is if you've ever been playing Vampire Survivors on console and thought "I wish my face was less comfortable."

For some odd reason it will not allow experience gems and coins to show up as collected in my collection 1.0. I’ve even erased all data and started over and still have the same issues so it impossible for me to unlock queen sigma and some other stages. To complete the game.

I am a BIG Vampire Survivors fan! I've played PC and Android versions many times over and love the format.

I have been wanting to play it in my headset for a while now, and finally I can!

If you have played the game in 2D before. This is the 2D Vampire Experience but with some added 3D elements that help build the universe.

You play in a 3D Environment that changes per map. So you feel like you're there.

You play essentially the 2D game. But each map has been turned into a 3D diorama.

Trees, bookshelves, tables, etc are all 3d.

Sprites and xp crystals are still 2d. But they are standing.

Attacks are 2d effects but are lifted and feel 3D in the space.

The game does a great job at bringing it out of 2d and into a 3d world! 

This includes 2 paid expansions and I believe all the up to date free expansions(though I have not reached this part yet, I believe the trailer showed clips)


Now...The game does require a little polish. Sometimes loading between menus feels sluggish. There is no pass through option. Some of the 3d assets don't have the same detail as others(the library bookshelfs look fantastic. But the piano, smaller bookshelves and tables feel blurry and kinda misty when observed at a closer view point). 

But other than the above, the game is amazing. And based on how Ponical has improved and expanded the base 2d game over the years I believe we will get the features people say are missing. 

Thank you SO much for bringing this into VR!

(As a suggestion,  bring the 2D version into VR too, then we can play it while in social VR worlds chatting with friends and having out)

This has made progression impossible. If you collect a rosary or time slow down item after the 15 min mark on that stage, the game will use the item on every frame and will not allow you to move. The game is in a glitched state and even if you manage to quit, it says you played on "Matt's forest".

I literally can't get the relic in the stage due to this and one run I specifically avoided all item pickups but the red death never comes to kill me. 

The game is fun otherwise, but it being unplayable this far in is not acceptable. I will change my rating if a patch addresses this.

developer_response.body: The bug with rosaries has been fixed in the latest update 🙂

reviews.review_description:
This is the first time in a LONG time that I've had so much fun with my Quest headset. There is absolutely a market for this kind of Hybrid VR game. I hope more developers follow suit (I would DIE for a Bloons TD game like this!).

I'm hoping that co-op (with crossplay!) gets added soon!

[UPDATE] Many issues have finally been fixed! Still needs more love, but you can actually progress through the "story" now!

It's Vampire Survivors and that's great.
There is missed potential to make it better:
- No passthrough, most of the action happens in a board-game style area. Passthrough for the room would have been perfect.
- No progress sync with PC/Mobile
- A 1st person view camera/mode/mini-game would have been nice

I've heard of Vampire Survivors but never played it myself. When I saw they released a VR version of it I figured why not try it out and see what all the buzz was about. Have to say, I absolutely get it now. This is an amazing game and honestly a must buy. As someone who plays a ton of VR games it's nice to have a good mix of first person high activity games and also these sit down games. This is easily my favorite sit down and play VR game. I can't speak for people who are veterans and have played various versions of this game but if you are new to Vampire Survivors this is a great version to own.

I wish they would put out more old school games like this. It's really quite time consuming. I love it but...the controls started glitching out on auto choose so I changed it to no pointer control. 
Which worked great until I got to the title screen again,now I'm locked out. It won't highlight anything and won't let me change the controls back. I erased and re-download but it still won't let me choose anything on the title screen. 
It's literally the only game I play for hours at a time. Please fix I'm halfway through unlocking characters.

It's vampire survivors, the full game, how!? It runs great, you can sit on the couch and play this with one controller, the board is like rotated 45 degrees and the sprites upright but some things like the scenery are 3d so it's not all flat sprites but everything pops and looks great. The backgrounds are full 3d and look great and stuff animates in the background. Kinda wish you could enable passthrough mode but then you'd miss out on the backgrounds. 10/10 port, time to grind the game once again!

UPDATE: Any and all problems I had with the game are now resolved. Great job by the devs!

The game is fantastic! The graphics are well ported and given a bit of a VR update, the changing backdrop environments are cool(though they should also include an MR option)
The game board pops wonderfully in 3D! 
Gameplay is fast and furious fun!

Get this game! It’s cheap and gives countless hours of solid gameplay.

reviews.review_title: Some hiccups on launch but good now
reviews.review_description:
I love VS and have it across multiple platforms. I was enjoying this port playing it on my bed before going to sleep.  All this came to a frustrating halt as Cappella Magna is broken on my Quest 3.  A couple weeks have gone by, things are moving again and all is well.  Gameplay is the same but now you can enjoy it with some diorama enhanced visualizations while being as comfy as possible.

From my original review, old bug which is now resolved: Approximately half-way through the level, picking up a rosary causes the game to essentially lock up. Even avoiding the rosary, the level will get stuck and not transition to the boss which effectively locks you out of progression.

reviews.review_title: Loving It Please Add More DLC
reviews.review_description: First Time Playing Vampire Survivors The 2.5 D Is Super Cool Please Add DLC I Would Pay Double For It
🚨Check out my full review on YouTube by searching ‘Gamertag VR’🚨

I never played any other version until the VR and I’m so hooked I got it on iPhone.  This hits all the right buttons (pun intended). Such a simple game yet I can’t put it down. 

MESSAGE TO DEVS: PLEASE RELEASE THE DLC! CASTELVANIA & CONTRA DLC 1st ;)


UPDATE 11/20/25: been experiencing a bunch of crashes and freezes once I hit the 15-20 min mark. The game froze on me 3 times in a row at roughly the same time. Please fix this. Game is unplayable for me right now. I’ll update this score back to 5 stars once it’s been resolved. 

Also, I’ve seen others mention the lack of MR, and I agree with them… this game seems like a no brainer MR title. Doesn’t affect my score of the game… just something this game could sorely use.

reviews.review_title: Awesome, the pop up gives a fresh feel
reviews.review_description:
Really well done converting the game to vr. Gives a great fresh feel to it. Should make it clear dlc is included thought was double the pc price for a moment.
I feel guilty asking for this feature since the environments are class, but pass through would be nice as well to play while TV on in background.
There's also a black bar cutting a bit of the horizontal fov on right eye by a small bit.

I had never heard of this game before. When it launched, I saw reviews for the flatscreen version which were highly praised. At only $10, I bought it immediately. After installation, the game has some bug. (black screen). I quit and restarted, and then it worked normally. After playing two rounds, I totally get why it's so popular. The VR presentation in this format is intriguing; you can see the characters clearly.

reviews.review_title: I hoped a little—but saw it coming.
reviews.review_description:
After finishing the review and coming back to add more, I want to make one thing clear: the game itself is genuinely fun. Of course it is—it’s not just a VS-like; it’s Vampire Survivors itself.
-
-
-
I had a faint hope when I started the game, but unfortunately, it turned out exactly as I feared. This isn’t even a “Vampire Survivors–like.” It is supposed to be Vampire Survivors itself—yet the way it has been brought over feels shockingly careless.

I had high expectations, especially because this is the official title, not an imitation, and I genuinely wanted to enjoy the VR version. But calling this a “VR port” is simply unacceptable. It feels like the developers fundamentally misunderstood what a VR adaptation should be. Nothing about this version takes advantage of VR in any meaningful or thoughtful way.

What makes this even more disappointing is the pricing. Despite launching on a different platform with the same $10 price tag as other complete and well-optimized versions, this VR release feels stripped down, rushed, and unworthy of being sold as a finished product. Releasing a version like this at full price is bound to alienate even the most dedicated supporters. The number of players who will continue to back the developers after this is going to be extremely small.

In short, this VR port does not do justice to the original game or to the potential of VR as a medium. It’s a major missed opportunity—and an experience I cannot recommend.

Was really happy to see this shadowdrop, my favorite steam deck game portable in another way!
But don't want to grind from scratch again, so sadly refunded until/if they add save game sync in.
Also really needs an option for passthrough instead of the background.

reviews.review_title: This game is way too hard
reviews.review_description:
The game’s totally underwhelming and full of bugs.
It’s no fun to play whatsoever. You gotta grind nonstop early on, and the late-game difficulty is straight-up unbeatable. Takes way too long, and there are way too few items. Don’t waste your money on this.

I like this game a lot but I have 2 issues with it. First and foremost, a game like this screams mixed reality. Honestly, I don't know why it would be released without it. Secondly (and this may just be me but somehow I doubt it), I am getting a black edge on my right side, obstructing my peripheral vision. It's not a game breaker or anything, but super annoying. If they could patch that out, that would be swell. Otherwise, good game overall, but if MR was added and that annoying problem is fixed, I will raise my rating for sure.

I had not played the original but was familiar with it.  I’m having a ton of fun with this version on my Quest 3.  I like the 2.5D effect - it gives a good retro feel.  There are a few bugs that I have seen mentioned in other reviews, but the developer has responded so that’s good to see.

Very enjoyable and pretty addictive. I have already sunk a bunch of hours into it haha.

reviews.review_title: A Classic With A Surprise VR Shadow Drop
reviews.review_description: A great game on flatscreen, a great game in VR!  For $10, it’s a great deal.  It looks…about how I would expect, and that’s part of it’s charm.  Definitely a different experience in VR, I like the background, I like the diorama style, it all just works.  And you know what?  It’s more fun than about half of the titles available in VR.  This is one of those games that I’ll probably end up buying five or six times on five or six devices.  This makes two.  More content would be nice down the road, but this is a great game as is at a nice price.
The game is super fun, but there is a bug where the field is pitch black when you start the game and it doesn't work. If that was fixed, it would be 5 stars.
It’s literally Vampire Survivors, but it’s missing multiplayer… it would be SO FUN, like couch co-op, but better!!!

The diorama style is very cool and love seeing the sprites and trees pop out from the playing field. Would love some mixed reality with pass-through and being able to place the board around your space.

I’m not sure what more a person would want out of this.  It’s a great VR version of an amazing game.  I would like more games like this on VR because the janky motion physic type games are mostly not that fun on VR.  The motion control novelty is like worn out since Wii Sports launched.  Just make more VR games like this where you can sit and play and have a blast with.

reviews.review_title: As of now, it is unplayable
reviews.review_description: Black screen after you choose a character and press start. Right eye has flashing lights

Honestly, I am a huge fan of these 2D games getting the VR treatment. The awe of seeing absurd abilities play out in front of you with the depth and experience of the world's most advanced and magical game board, experiencing things such as lightning raining down from above is exactly the magical experience VR was made to enable, and an oddly hard one to communicate to others who have not properly experienced it, and thus, I highly recommend giving it a go! This game is bang for buck, hours of addictive content - Additionally, Vampire Survivors VR runs great, and is easy to sit down and enjoy, with settings for those who struggle with motion sickness (while already being motion sickness friendly innately).

This is a very serviceable version of the game with an interesting perspective and lovely 3D background. 
But it plays better with a paired hand controller rather than using a Quest controller. Unfortunately with my 8BitDo controller, support is hit and miss, even when telling it to become a dedicated controller. It often refuses to acknowledge menu inputs, or even allow power up selections. 
With better controller support, this would be an ace version.

I own the game on multiple platforms and was curious how it would be in vr. The results were mixed. The 3d and assets are nice. The fact you get the first 2 dlc included was nice. But no licaned dlc or cloud save to sync progress so fresh start. The interface need work but overall works well retaining the simple clean style you expect. Just minor issues with elements hidden under other bits or your cursor going black when the background goes black. Nothing you can't overlook. Loading can be a bit slow at times. 

Overall worth a buy at $10 but not my first choice if I had a mobile or switch version handy on the go or PC if I want all the content or responsiveness when things get frenetic after fully unlocking the game.
I only gave it 3 stars because it sometimes fails to start the mission and I've had the game freeze on me multiple times, ruining my run.

I never heard of Vampire Survivors (on any platform) before, but checked out IGN and PC Gamer reviews of the flat game and was intrigued so gave it a shot to see what all the fuss was about, given the low price of entry.

On paper it sounds like it shouldn't be fun, walking around while auto aiming shoots out random bullets at enemies, but when I put on the headset at 10pm and discovered I'd been playing till 3am I realised I was hooked 😅 

The risk reward mechanic works brilliantly!

VR doesn't add too much to the experience apart from cool visual effects in the background while you are playing like bats flying past you, but the single best thing about having it on the Quest is the ability to lie back in bed looking up at the ceiling with arms resting to your sides and just let your brain get immersed in the game while feeling totally zen.

ONE SMALL SUGGESTION FOR THE DEVS: when you collect a chest and the "Done" button appears I should be able to press a button on the controller to close it, even if the controller is resting to my sides when lying down. At the moment I need to point the controller at the button to close it, whereas all other interactions in the game work while the controller is resting at the side of my body.

UPDATE: OK so since writing this review I discovered a setting in the Options that allows you to control the game without "pointing" so now I can finally close all menus by just pressing a button on the controller without having to raise my arm to point at anything 😅 

I also got a chance to try VS on my phone just to compare and it's not nearly as much fun dragging your finger across the screen as this version. So this game deserves 120% score if I could give it!

reviews.review_title: Best survivor game ever gets an upgrade
reviews.review_description: The 2.5D graphics on this works! It has a Paper Mario  like presentation. I'm falling in love with this game all over again.

reviews.review_title: Most Addictive Game now in VR!
reviews.review_description: Search YouTube for Paradise Decay Vampire Survivors VR for gameplay and my 1st Impressions of the game.

reviews.review_title: One of the most addictive games now be
reviews.review_description: It awesome just like the flat version but you can move the playing field which is diagonally tilted in front of you. You can move it around and place it closer or further from you face. I like moving it all the way up. The game just needs the current flcs then I’ll be the vast way to play it.

The creators of vampire survivors are really cool when it comes to the price, and I have to say this is an incredible start to vampire survivors on the v r. Is it perfect? Absolutely , not , but it doesn't need much work to be really incredible. I absolutely love it !!
bring the dlc well buy it!!!!!

You play as a player that lives in a VR environment who plays the game on a flat screen.
I would prefer you to just play it on a normal console or PC. 

The game itself is crazy fun. But that has ZERO relation to VR.
Time to spend another 10 days on this amazing game, but this time IN VR!
It's Vampire Survivor on VR. Really fun rogue-like for. Don't go thinking this is at all different from other iterations like mobile; it is the same just playable on VR. And that is cool.

reviews.review_title: Would be perfect if it had MR
reviews.review_description: MR mode doesn't have to be anchored to a surface or table. It could be just simple passthrough, so I can check my surroundings while at the laundromat.

reviews.review_title: Best way to play vampire survivors!
reviews.review_description: Love this, please add the DLC!!

After sinking over 40 hours in Vampire Survivors I'm set to do it all again.
It's simplistic and perfect. Standing up sprites on the playfield elevates this game past god tier.
Every level has a unique environment you play in and they have little hints snuck in when new waves come which is such a neat little touch.
More games need this treatment.
I hope the DLC gets added on, playing the Ode to Castlevania this way would be amazing.
This is the path VR should take.

Vampire Survivors is, in my opinion, one of the best games of 2022. This is that same fantastic game, just in VR. This port is a little rough around the edges, and definitely needs some polish, but it is still tons of fun to play and I highly recommend it.
Really happy this is finally in virtual reality!  We just need this to now be in mixed reality!
I love everything about it! But I'm hoping for a mixed reality mode.
I very much likey
This is the best survival game I’ve I like how it’s got evos and doesn’t just stay at 8th upgrade
All the addictive qualities that made the original great are here, just in VR. It's a little buggy and does freeze here and there, but it's worth it to play the game in VR
Not virtual reality just a sad flat port

reviews.review_title: Oh help me, please, I can't stop!
reviews.review_description:
Addiction in it's purest form...
Though you might want to look into 50% of the games starting with a black screen which requires a game restart.

I am a huge fan of this game on the pc and I really like the 3d perspective that has been implemented here. It’s fantastic and plays great. This and pinball vr are my current greatest games ever on my quest 3.
Good game
Just chillin with one button game, perfect camera point, love this game.
The game is really fun. Unfortunately, the VR version doesn't have cross-save, which I find a real shame since I play it on every platform.
Was disapointed as i expected a vr mode. But its the same game as on other platforms
Of course it's the addictive, time destroying hell of a bullet game now in VR. Great to get lost for a few hours.
Love the original on PS5 and love this port also! It’s fun turning on “play laying down” in the quest menu and playing it laying on my back looking up.
Bug on stage five prevents you from progressing, the final boss Wintour appear.

developer_response.body: We have released a fix for this issue today 🧄

reviews.review_description: This is the 5st time I by the game on a different councils.

This is the best version of Vampire Survivors so far and it came from nowhere to me. This game is so much fun and totally adictive, so careful when you play it.

For the devs, I'm experiencing an annoying bug at the start of some runs when I get a mostly black screen with some buttons, making the run unabble to play. Aways this happens I need to quit and try to restart the run.

Besisdes that the game runs flawlessly.

It’s basically a 2D Roguelite done as a tabletop 3D-ish game in a fully rendered vr environment that matches the level you’re playing. 

Tons of unlockables. Infinite replayability. Endless fun!

Play seated with one controller. Comfortable, but never chill. You can relax while you grind.

Was excited to see this, tho i thought it eas going to be a first person immersion, but it's pretty much a port from the steam version...

It may not be different enough if you already have it on a different platform to get here...

Using the thumbstick takes a little getting used to as does the perspective, but overall pretty faithful...

I did get an xbox controller to try and use with the hame but it doesn't seem to work, or i cant figure it out ..

It's alright. Of course we would all like 6 dof motion controls in first person but let's be honest it's not happening with this game. But I do like playing it in the 3D environment. And I find it more of a challenge than on PC etc for some reason. Which is cool. Yeah I don't mind it. Hope they add more DLC.

reviews.review_title: Can’t not give a 3/5 game 5/5 coz of ❤️
reviews.review_description: I can’t help but be unreasonable and fully know I am being so when it’s concerning such a beloved piece of media that I feel truly attached to as with VS. I have clocked up over 500 hours on my Steam Deck version of VS. This isn’t perfect, but it will get there because my faith in this I.P is as it is with ‘Star Wars’ and everything ‘Rockstar Games’ has ever knocked out. You don’t criticise these franchises in my presence because I’ll take it as a personal insult (for a wildly unfair and ridiculously illogical reason I’ll randomly attach to it).
Honestly,  I haven't even played it yet.  It just existing is enough reason to give it 5 stars.

I loved vampire survivors when I tried it, I like this version, but I wish there was a first person option, Thats what I expected, but I do recommend this if you want a portable Vampire Survivors



-The Monk vr offical
I was skeptical about how this was going to work but they nailed the tabletop aesthetic. Runs well and doesnt give me flashbacks to other peoples memories while playing which is a nice feature.
Great game in vr
my headset must have been bugging out. restarted and crashes seem gone. great fun game

This a great simple port, just played until my battery died. 
Please, pllllleeeeeeease add multiplayer
Next level of fun
Excellent
